mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion and construction sector acceleration, which jointly boost corporate profit forecasts and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over 60-day horizons in 70% of triggered periods, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equity markets and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of periods where INDPRO increases, or the mechanism Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 during early-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive relationship with payroll increases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of high-inflation trigger months, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand across production and consumption channels, which reinforces earnings upgrade cycles and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in multivariate regression, or 20-day forward returns underperform the market by more than 0.5% in 65% of co-occurring growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% while CPI crosses above 2.5%, the combination signals an overheating economy with constrained policy room, triggering short-term risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods meeting the dual threshold, or the regime-specific predictive power vanishes when tested against a neutral macro baseline.
